ddls:
CREATE TABLE "performance_metrics" (
  "metric_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each performance metric record */,
  "node_id" INTEGER /* ID of the compute node */,
  "float_adds" TEXT /* Number of floating point additions */,
  "float_mult" TEXT /* Number of floating point multiplications */,
  "float_div" TEXT /* Number of floating point divisions */,
  "float_comp" TEXT /* Number of floating point comparisons */,
  "float_total_math" TEXT /* Total number of floating point operations (excluding comparisons) */,
  "float_total_with_comp" TEXT /* Total number of floating point operations (including comparisons) */,
  "total_runtime" TEXT /* Total runtime in microseconds */,
  "performance" REAL /* Performance metric (excluding comparisons) */,
  "performance_with_comp" REAL /* Performance metric (including comparisons) */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the metric was recorded */,
  "power_consumption" REAL /* Power consumption of the node in watts */,
  "temperature" REAL /* Temperature of the node in degrees Celsius */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("metric_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "power_consumption" (
  "power_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each power consumption record */,
  "node_id" INTEGER /* ID of the compute node */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp when the power usage was recorded */,
  "power_usage" REAL /* Power usage of the node in watts */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("power_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "temperature_logs" (
  "temp_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each temperature log record */,
  "node_id" INTEGER /* ID of the compute node */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp when the temperature was recorded */,
  "temperature" REAL /* Temperature of the node in degrees Celsius */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("temp_id")
)
